question.text: What happens to a trader's open position if they don't close it before the expiry of the contract?

answer.text: The position is settled at the final settlement price (FBIL reference rate) through cash settlement
The position is automatically rolled over to the next month's contract
The trader must take physical delivery of the currency
The position is cancelled with no profit or loss

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Positions open at expiry are marked to the Final Settlement Price (FBIL reference rate) and cash settled in INR. There is no automatic rollover or physical delivery. **The Trap:** Exchange-traded currency futures do NOT auto-rollover. If you want to maintain a position, you must manually close the expiring contract and open a new one. **Cross-Exam Context:** Expiry settlement is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Traders often roll positions a few days before expiry when the near-month contract's liquidity starts declining.
